name: Similarity as Reward Alignment: Robust and Versatile Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
summary: arXiv:2506.12529v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (PbRL) entails a variety of approaches for aligning models with human intent to alleviate the burde...
description: arXiv:2506.12529v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (PbRL) entails a variety of approaches for aligning models with human intent to alleviate the burden of reward engineering. However, most previous PbRL work has not investigated the robustness to labeler errors, inevitable with labelers who are non-experts or operate under time constraints. We intr...
author: Sara Rajaram, R. James Cotton, Fabian H. Sinz
